candidate.name: fused_rank16_scalar_simple
candidate.strategy: Slight latency reduction from fusing the rank-16 update (A @ (B.T @ X)) into a single lightweight kernel, reducing launch overhead and intermediate memory traffic.

candidate.name: fused_rank16_scalar_simple
candidate.strategy: Slight latency reduction from fusing the rank-16 update (A @ (B.T @ X)) into a single lightweight kernel, reducing launch overhead and intermediate memory traffic.

avoid: custom GEMM for W @ X – unlikely to beat cuBLAS
hardcoding hidden dimension d or any tensor sizes
introducing correctness risk by over‑optimizing the full matmul
evidence: aggregate speedup is 0.9996, essentially identical to PyTorch baseline
profile summary explicitly identifies multiple GEMM and add launches as bottleneck
correctness is perfect across all three shapes
recommended_actions: keep cuBLAS for the large W @ X matmul since it is already optimal
fuse B.T @ X and A @ (result) into a single kernel to reduce launch overhead and intermediate memory traffic
use vectorized float4 loads/stores for the rank‑16 update to improve memory bandwidth
consider a tiled shared memory strategy for the rank‑16 kernel if fusion alone is insufficient

warnings: No check that d is within the specified range [3584, 4608], but the code will work for any d.
In-place modification of temporary tensor y.add_(...) is acceptable but may be suboptimal for some autograd uses.

suggested_actions: fuse rank-16 update into the output tensor
test scalar and vectorized rank-16 kernels
